title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement Mariel Sorlien. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Introduction to R
text: R free, open source coding language. frequently used data analysis, although can also used build websites R Shiny.

title: Introduction to R
text: RStudio free integrated development environment (IDE), provides user-friendly interface create maintain R projects. run RStudio, must also download R.

title: Introduction to R
text: default RStudio layout includes four windows, multiple tabs. important tabs locations shown : brief overview tab : Source: View, edit, run R scripts Console: Type R commands. Also displays messages, warnings, errors code run. Environment: View stored variables. Files: View folders files. Add, open, rename, delete files . Packages: Install update R packages. Viewer: View rendered files. can customize R Studio’s appearance behavior Tools > Global Options.

title: Introduction to R
text: Every RStudio project must placed folder, WQdashboard exception. download WQdashboard github, receive zipped folder. unzip folder, rename move files folders unless experienced R. following files folders especially important: data data-raw DESCRIPTION inst R tests wqdashboard.Rproj open WQdashboard RStudio, open wqdashboard.Rproj. WQdashboard folder shown “Files” tab. files need add data run WQdashboard can found data-raw folder. scripts data-raw numbered order run include detailed instructions top file. first time using WQdashboard, run 00_install.R anything else. run script, use CTRL + SHIFT + ENTER Windows computer CMD + SHIFT + ENTER Mac. IMPORTANT “Run” button top source window run current line selected text, entire script.

title: Introduction to R
text: Due open source nature, R’s capabilities can greatly expanded downloading packages containing custom code. WQdashboard uses multiple packages, including R Shiny, importwqd, wqformat. WQdashboard can run unless packages installed. download required packages, run data-raw/00_install.R. Packages may updated time time order fix bugs add new features. prompted update packages, . manually install update packages, select “Packages” tab lower right window. “Install” “Update” buttons can used add update packages.

title: Publishing
text: several different ways share share custom dashboard online, including shinyapps.io, posit connect, shiny server. smaller organizations, recommend shinyapps.io straightforward use free tier includes 25 hours active use per month.

title: Publishing
text: Make account shinyapps.io shinyapps, go Account > Tokens. Select Show, Show Secret copy provided code. Return RStudio/Posit Connect paste code console window. Hit ENTER run code. Run data-raw/08_launch.R Shinyapps host dashboard standalone website. can find link dashboard “Recent Applications” main page, Applications > .

title: Publishing
text: WQdashboard can embedded website iframe. Simply copy code replace “LINK” link dashboard. height, width, title can adjusted needed. WQdashboard load whenever page embedded opened. concerned using many active hours, embed WQdashboard page instead homepage.
code: <iframe src="LINK" title="Water Quality Dashboard" width="100%" height = "800"></iframe>

title: WQdashboard
text: WQdashboard open-source dashboard template can used display water quality data using interactive maps, report cards, graphs. designed water quality organizations want share data public. WQdashboard written R Shiny. coding knowledge required set run WQdashboard, although advanced users can take advantage modular code fully customize WQdashboard meet needs. new R, please read vignette("intro_to_r") instructions programs download use .

title: WQdashboard
text: Although WQdashboard native data format, accepts multiple input formats site, result, threshold data. Templates WQdashboard’s format can found inst/extdata folder. Supported formats listed .

title: WQdashboard
text: Unsupported data formats can still used long tables long wide, eg observation placed row. use custom format, fill csv files data-raw/custom_format.

title: WQdashboard
text: data-raw folder contains series numbered scripts. scripts must run order, although steps 0-4 need rerun initial setup. Steps 2, 4, 6 optional. script contains detailed description . scripts also include variables description. running script, edit variables changing text right <-. text quotes except special values NA, TRUE, FALSE. Example run script, use CTRL + SHIFT + ALT Windows computer CMD + SHIFT + ALT Mac.

title: WQdashboard
text: script installs packages needed run WQdashboard. also installs tinytex, used generate PDFs.

title: WQdashboard
text: script used customize site theme, “” page, “Download” page. running script, open inst/app/www folder update following files: .qmd, Download.qmd, _brand.yml .qmd sets content “” page. contains default text can replaced. Download.qmd sets content “Download” page; dashboard , “Download” button placed contents Download.qmd. Download.qmd contains default text suggested citation light code dynamically update organization name latest year updated; feel free replace elements static text remove entirely. .qmd Download.qmd Quarto documents. unfamiliar Quarto, can select “Visual” mode upper left corner document window edit documents way Word document. may add images .qmd Download.qmd long stored inst/app/www folder. Tables also fully supported. IMPORTANT change header either Quarto document. IMPORTANT Changes .qmd Download.qmd reflected website run 01_customize_website.R. _brand.yml lets customize appearance site. Set line 2 (name:) name organization line 3 (title:) desired website title. remaining lines set color palette fonts website. like learn custom themes, visit _brand.yml website. IMPORTANT must update line 2 (name:) organization name ensure organization properly credited “Download” “Report Card” tabs.

title: WQdashboard
text: Optional. like add custom watershed river layers interactive map, use script upload shapefiles. Shapefiles must stored data-raw folder. Shapefiles must use WGS 1984 projection. Higher resolution shapefiles take longer load may slow website.

title: WQdashboard
text: watershed_shp: Name watershed shapefile. Must polygon layer. Set NA want upload shapefile. watershed_name_col: Watershed column name. column used label watershed polygon. river_shp: Name river shapefile. Must polyline layer. Set NA want upload shapefile. river_name_col: River column name. column used label river polyline.

title: WQdashboard
text: script uploads site metadata. Site metadata must saved csv file data-raw folder. See Supported Data Formats list supported formats. use custom format, set in_format variable “custom” fill file: data-raw/custom_format/colnames_sites.csv like use official WQdashboard format, template can found inst/extdata/template_sites.csv

title: WQdashboard
text: sites_csv: Name csv file containing site metadata. in_format: Input format. case sensitive. Options: WQdashboard, WQX, MassWateR, RI_WW, MA_BRC, ME_FOCB, custom default_state: State name abbreviation. blank values “State” column updated value. Set NA leave blanks -.

title: WQdashboard
text: Optional. Thresholds used determine whether parameter values classified excellent, good, fair, poor. default, WQdashboard includes several thresholds based state federal regulations. thresholds, however, highly site specific - example, Rhode Island water quality regulations dictate turbidity exceed 5 NTU background values class AA water. account regulations, WQdashboard lets users add site, group, /state specific thresholds. include custom thresholds, fill custom threshold template data-raw/thresholds.csv. copy file can found inst/extdata/template_thresholds.csv needed. adding custom thresholds, threshold placed row. columns mean: State, Group, Site_ID: columns determine site(s) threshold applies . row may include Group Site_ID . three columns blank, threshold applied sites. Depth_Category: OPTIONAL. want threshold apply specific depth category, specify . f column left blank, threshold applied depth levels. Values: Surface, Midwater, Near Bottom, Bottom Parameter, Unit: Parameter, unit. Calculation: Method used calculate annual numeric score. Acceptable values: minimum, maximum, average, median, geometric mean, 90th percentile Threshold_Min, Threshold_Max: minimum maximum acceptable values. Must enter value least one column. Excellent, Good, Fair: threshold cut values excellent, good, fair results. Either leave three columns blank fill three . multiple thresholds provided location, depth, parameter, thresholds applied lowest score used. Example table , Enterococcus must annual geometric mean 35 cfu/100mL 90th percentile 130 cfu/100mL else assigned score “Meet Criteria.” Total Coliform, however, needs 90th percentile 100 cfu/100mL.

title: WQdashboard
text: skip_step: Set TRUE skip step go next file. Set FALSE like add custom thresholds. threshold_csv: Name csv file containing threshold metadata. in_format: Format used parameters units. case sensitive Accepted values: WQdashboard, WQX, MassWateR, RI_DEM, RI_WW, MA_BRC, ME_DEP, ME_FOCB, Custom. using custom format, update following files: data-raw/custom_format/varnames_parameters.csv data-raw/custom_format/varnames_units.csv

title: WQdashboard
text: script adds updates result data. numeric results accepted; categorical results uploaded using 06_add_categorical_results.csv. Result data must saved csv file data-raw folder. Multiple data formats supported (see Supported Data Formats). template official WQdashboard format can found inst/extdata/template_results.csv using unsupported/custom format, set in_format “custom” update following files: data-raw/custom_format/colnames_results.csv data-raw/custom_format/varnames_activity.csv data-raw/custom_format/varnames_parameters.csv data-raw/custom_format/varnames_qualifiers.csv data-raw/custom_format/varnames_units.csv

title: WQdashboard
text: results_csv: Name CSV file containing result data. in_format: Input format. case sensitive. Accepted values: WQdashboard, WQX, MassWateR, RI_DEM, RI_WW, MA_BRC, ME_DEP, ME_FOCB, custom date_format: Format used “Date” column. Example: “m/d/y”. List abbreviations: B - Full month name (August) b - Abbreviated month name (Aug) m - Month, numeric (8) d - Day month y - Year without century (26) Y - Year century (2026) H - Hour m - Minute S - Second p - /PM z - Timezone timezone: Timezone. overwrite_existing: TRUE, replaces old result data new data. FALSE, combines old new result data. recalculate_score: TRUE, recalculates parameter scores, including old data. FALSE, recalculate old scores. update_citation: TRUE, update “year_updated” variable Download.qmd

title: WQdashboard
text: Optional. script adds updates categorical /download result data. data available download, display maps, report card, graph tabs. Categorical result data must saved csv file data-raw folder. Multiple data formats supported (see Supported Data Formats). template official WQdashboard format can found inst/extdata/template_results.csv using unsupported/custom format, set in_format “custom” update following files: data-raw/custom_format/colnames_results.csv data-raw/custom_format/varnames_activity.csv data-raw/custom_format/varnames_parameters.csv data-raw/custom_format/varnames_qualifiers.csv data-raw/custom_format/varnames_units.csv

title: WQdashboard
text: results_csv: Name CSV file containing result data. in_format: Input format. case sensitive. Accepted values: WQdashboard, WQX, MassWateR, RI_DEM, RI_WW, MA_BRC, ME_DEP, ME_FOCB, custom date_format: Format used “Date” column. Example: “m/d/y”. List abbreviations: B - Full month name (August) b - Abbreviated month name (Aug) m - Month, numeric (8) d - Day month y - Year without century (26) Y - Year century (2026) H - Hour m - Minute S - Second p - /PM z - Timezone timezone: Timezone. overwrite_existing: TRUE, replaces old categorical result data new data. FALSE, combines old new categorical result data. update_citation: TRUE, update “year_updated” variable Download.qmd

title: WQdashboard
text: script launches WQdashboard local computer lets preview looks. step always run 08_launch.R order check bugs.

title: WQdashboard
text: Run script create update WQdashboard website. order host WQdashboard shinyapps.io posit connect, MUST create account first. NOTE: added custom thresholds, shapefiles, categorical data, may receive warning message undocumented variables. message can safely ignored, want stop seeing , go R/data.R uncomment section describing relevant file(s) removing first # start line. (can also select relevant section use CTRL + SHIFT + C) information, see vignette("publishing").

text: launch_to: upload website. Options: “shinyapps.io”, “posit connect”, “shiny server” create_docker_file: Whether generate tar.gz file can used install app locally.

text: WQdashboard data can updated time running appropriate scripts. adding modifying data, run 07_preview.R check bugs 08_launch.R update website.

text: update “” “Download” page, run 01_customize_website.R. Changes _brand.yml automatically applied, even 01_customize_website.R run.

title: WQdashboard
text: update site metadata, run 03_add_sites.R. overwrite existing site metadata.

title: WQdashboard
text: update custom thresholds, run 04_add_thresholds.R. overwrite existing custom thresholds. ensure new thresholds properly applied prior results data, run 05_add_results.R recalculate_score set TRUE.

title: WQdashboard
text: Run 05_add_results.R /06_add_categorical_results.R add new result data. overwrite_existing TRUE, overwrite previous results, overwrite_existing FALSE, new results added old results without overwriting .

title: Water Quality Dashboard
text: WQdashboard open-source dashboard template can used display water quality data using interactive maps, report cards, graphs. coding knowledge required! tool accepts multiple different data formats, including used WQX, RI DEM, Watershed Watch, Blackstone River Coalition. WQdashboard designed help water quality organizations share data standalone embedded website.

title: Water Quality Dashboard
text: GitHub account, select “Use template” create new repository. Else, select “Code” > “Download ZIP” download code. Please read following vignettes instructions using WQdashboard: General Overview Introduction R Publishing

title: Run the Shiny Application — run_app
text: Run Shiny Application

title: Run the Shiny Application — run_app
code: run_app(   onStart = NULL,   options = list(),   enableBookmarking = NULL,   uiPattern = "/",   ... )

title: Run the Shiny Application — run_app
text: onStart function called app actually run. needed shinyAppObj, since shinyAppDir case, global.R file can used purpose. options Named options passed runApp call (can following: "port", "launch.browser", "host", "quiet", "display.mode" "test.mode"). can also specify width height parameters provide hint embedding environment ideal height/width app. enableBookmarking Can one "url", "server", "disable". default value, NULL, respect setting previous calls  enableBookmarking(). See enableBookmarking() information bookmarking app. uiPattern regular expression applied GET request determine whether ui used handle request. Note entire request path must match regular expression order match considered successful. ... arguments pass golem_opts. See `?golem::get_golem_options` details.

title: Default UI dropdown values — varlist
text: list default values various dropdown menus sidebar graph tab.

title: Default UI dropdown values — varlist
text: list 13 columns: state List states town List towns watershed List watersheds site_id List site IDs site_name List site names loc_choices List toggle options. Possible values: "blank",    "Town" = "town", "State" = "town", "Watershed" = "watershed" loc_tab Name hidden tab importwqd:::mod_sidebar_location.    Possible values: "notoggle", "toggle", "blank" param List parameters param_score List parameters assigned score param_cat List download /categorical parameters depth List depths. Possible values: Surface, Midwater,    Near Bottom, Bottom year List years month List months
